hero_summary: Farahnak is a branch of the Sikah family, and it becomes clearly colored in the upper region, giving the family a different expressive route.
short_identity: A Sikah-family branch with a clear upper color and a different expressive route.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic according to the chosen root
quick_facts.ghammaz: Its importance varies according to the practical route
quick_facts.character: Subtle, colored, and different in the jawab
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by stabilizing the tonic, then opens the upper region

what_is_it: This branch is understood inside the Sikah family as a maqam that preserves the essence of the Sikah tonic, but develops a clear upper color and a different expressive route.

lower.name: Jins Sikah
lower.description: The lower Sikah jins remains the basis that ties this branch to the family.

upper.name: Distinctively colored upper region
upper.starts_on: After the tonic has been stabilized
upper.description: Its personality appears through the special color in the upper region.

jins_architecture.transition_note: The difference from basic Sikah appears in the upper coloring and in the way the return is handled.

sayr.summary: It confirms the tonic first, then develops an upper route with its own special color before returning to reaffirm family belonging.
resting_tones: Resting points within the lower jins
Upper stations specific to this branch
motion_notes: The difference from Sikah is determined through the upper color
One should listen to how the jawab is opened and how the return from it is shaped
common_path: Reveal the branch’s special upper color

transpose_explainer: When this branch is transposed to another root, the interval logic and the functions of the tones that give it its identity inside the Sikah family must be preserved.
historical_notes: This branch is understood through its relation to the parent maqam and through its difference from it in the upper region.
school_differences: Its explanation may differ from one school or reference to another.
common_confusions: Listing note names alone is not enough to understand this branch; one must pay attention to movement and repose.
listening_notes: Begin by comparing it with basic Sikah.
Notice where the special color appears in the upper region.
